title: Chapter 1 Things Every Driver Should Know

mcq.q: According to the excerpt, what is a common reaction of those who are mechanically inclined, observed by those who are not?
options: Admiration
Exasperation
Amazement
All of the above

mcq.q: What does the excerpt suggest about acquiring mechanical skills?
options: It requires innate talent
It requires expensive tools
It can be learned by anyone
It is only useful for experienced mechanics

mcq.q: What is the first step mentioned in the excerpt to a driver being able to check oil or coolant levels?
options: Changing a tire
Jacking up the vehicle
Opening the hood
Filling the fuel tank

mcq.q: According to the excerpt, why is it suggested that drivers fill their own fuel tanks?
options: It’s faster
It’s safer
It’s cheaper
It’s more convenient

mcq.q: According to the text, what is one benefit of doing things yourself?
options: A. Spending more money
B. Gaining a sense of power
C. Requiring expensive tools
D. Feeling embarrassed

mcq.q: Before undertaking a job, the author suggests asking which of the following questions?
options: A. Is it the latest trend?
B. Do I really want to do it?
C. Can I get a discount?
D. How many friends can I invite?

mcq.q: What did the author initially believe about her inability to remove a bolt?
options: A. She lacked the right tools.
B. It was due to her being female.
C. She was rushing the process.
D. The bolt was inherently defective.

mcq.q: According to the text, what is more important than size or sex when it comes to moving difficult objects?
options: A. Using the right tools.
B. Focusing strength and technique.
C. Asking for help from big guys.
D. Having a long handle.

mcq.q: What did the author learn from watching the 'big guys' in her auto class?
options: A. That strength is primarily about physical size.
B. That patience comes from knowing you're stronger than others.
C. That focusing strength and perseverance leads to success.
D. That it's always best to ask for help.

mcq.q: According to the excerpt, what is the most annoying part of replacing vehicle parts?
options: A. Finding the correct tools.
B. Needing the vehicle to drive back to the store for exchanges.
C. Reading the owner’s manual.
D. Disabling the vehicle.

mcq.q: Where can you typically find vehicle specifications?
options: A. Only in the service manual.
B. Only on metal tags or decals.
C. In the owner's manual, on metal tags/decals, or from the parts department.
D. Only at auto supply stores.

mcq.q: What does “specs” refer to in the context of vehicle parts?
options: A. The color of the paint on the vehicle.
B. Vehicle specifications or requirements.
C. The type of tools needed for the repair.
D. The location of the radiator.

mcq.q: What does OEM stand for?
options: A. Original Equipment Manufacturer
B. Owner's Equipment Manual
C. Overall Engineering Method
D. Only Extremely Modern

mcq.q: Where can you find specifications for buying and gapping spark plugs if they're not found elsewhere?
options: A. Chapter 1
B. Chapter 5
C. A “Tune-Up Specification Guide”
D. The glove compartment.

mcq.q: According to the excerpt, why might a person ask for a discount when buying car parts?
options: A. They are a professional mechanic.
B. They plan to resell the parts.
C. They are a student.
D. They are installing the parts themselves.

mcq.q: What is the primary benefit of installing car parts yourself, as stated in the excerpt?
options: A. Getting discounts at auto parts stores.
B. Avoiding labor charges.
C. Learning new skills.
D. Improving the car's performance.

mcq.q: What is the main reason the excerpt cautions against storing or carrying gasoline?
options: A. Gasoline is difficult to transport.
B. Gasoline is flammable and can create an explosive vapor.
C. Gasoline damages the car's engine.
D. Gasoline is expensive.

mcq.q: What is the recommended method for safely disposing of an old gasoline can?
options: A. Pouring it down the drain.
B. Burning it in a controlled environment.
C. Filling it with water and recycling it.
D. Burying it in the backyard.

mcq.q: Why does the excerpt advise against using gasoline to clean car parts?
options: A. It’s less effective than other solvents.
B. It’s too expensive.
C. It’s safer than mechanic’s solvent.
D. It works better and has a flame retardant.

mcq.q: According to the text, why should you pull on wrenches rather than push them?
options: A. It’s easier on your wrists.
B. To avoid the possibility of your hand hitting something if the part suddenly comes loose.
C. It’s a faster way to loosen stuck parts.
D. It prevents the wrench from slipping.

mcq.q: What is the primary reason for removing jewelry before working on a vehicle?
options: A. To avoid scratches on the vehicle’s surface.
B. To prevent the jewelry from getting lost.
C. To avoid the jewelry getting caught on parts and causing damage.
D. To improve dexterity.

mcq.q: Why is it particularly important to store gasoline in a specifically designed can?
options: A. To improve the gasoline's quality.
B. Because the vapor in an empty can is explosive.
C. To prevent leaks and spills.
D. To make the can easier to carry.

mcq.q: According to the text, where is information about buying a work light located?
options: A. Chapter 1
B. Chapter 2
C. Chapter 3
D. The sidebar on gasoline disposal.

mcq.q: Why is it recommended to work in a well-ventilated area?
options: A. To keep the area cool.
B. To avoid breathing in carbon monoxide and toxic fumes.
C. To improve visibility.
D. To reduce noise.

mcq.q: According to the text, why is it beneficial to fill your gas tank in the morning?
options: A. It's cheaper during morning hours.
B. Gasoline expands with heat, reducing energy content.
C. Gas stations offer discounts in the morning.
D. Morning fuel deliveries are of higher quality.

mcq.q: At what temperature are service station sales meters set?
What is the primary safety concern regarding open flames near a gas pump?
options: A. The flame will damage the pump.
B. It could cause an explosion.
C. It might attract unwanted attention.
D. It can cause static electricity.

mcq.q: What should you do if a previous sale is registered on the price window of the pump?
options: A. Immediately report it to the attendant.
B. Ignore it and proceed with pumping gas.
C. Reset it by following the next steps to activate the pump.
D. Assume the price is incorrect and choose a different pump.

mcq.q: What is the purpose of the rubber vapor-recovery sleeves on some nozzles?
options: A. To measure fuel flow more accurately.
B. To allow fuel to flow.
C. To prevent gasoline spillage.
D. To reduce noise during pumping.

mcq.q: According to the excerpt, why is it dangerous to “top off” a fuel tank?
options: A. It always damages the fuel pump.
B. It may cause fuel to overflow or leak, creating a fire hazard and polluting the environment.
C. It prevents the tank from sealing properly.
D. It voids the vehicle’s warranty.

mcq.q: What is the recommended course of action if you are unable to locate the hood release?
options: A. Attempt to force the hood open.
B. Consult a mechanic immediately.
C. Ask a gas station attendant to show you how to open it.
D. Ignore the issue and avoid opening the hood.

mcq.q: Where is the hood release often found in newer vehicle models?
options: A. Behind the grill or bumper.
B. Inside the vehicle, near the steering column or driver’s seat.
C. Underneath the car, near the engine.
D. On the dashboard, near the radio.

mcq.q: What does the text suggest as a cost-effective alternative to paying for an attendant to show you how to open the hood?
options: A. Attempt to fix it yourself without consulting the owner's manual.
B. Purchase a specialized tool to open the hood.
C. Request only $5 worth of gasoline.
D. Call a friend for assistance.

mcq.q: What potential benefits are mentioned if you ask an attendant to show you how to open the hood?
options: A. Free oil changes and tire rotations.
B. A discount on fuel and a complimentary car wash and tire pressure check.
C. A free diagnostic test of the engine.
D. A detailed explanation of the vehicle's maintenance schedule.

mcq.q: According to the excerpt, what should you do initially when trying to open a car hood?
options: Tighten the hood shocks
Press the brakes
Find a handle, lever, arm, or button
Check the wheel bearings

mcq.q: What is the purpose of the safety catch mentioned in the text?
options: To lubricate the wheel bearings
To prevent the hood from opening accidentally
To secure the hood prop
To check the brakes

mcq.q: What does the excerpt suggest doing if you encounter difficulties while working on a task?
options: Panic and rush through it
Have some water or coffee
Check the wheel bearings
Tighten the hood shocks

mcq.q: What is a hood prop?
options: A gas-pressurized cylinder
A long, thin metal rod
A metal lever
A flat tire

mcq.q: What should you do if the hood doesn't feel secure and the excerpt suspects gas leakage?
options: Check the wheel bearings
Secure the hood with a broom handle
Tighten the hood shocks
Lubricate the brakes

mcq.q: According to the excerpt, why shouldn't some bolts be tightened too securely?
options: A. To prevent parts from flying off.
B. To ensure the engine runs smoothly.
C. To avoid stripping bolt threads or squeezing gaskets out of shape.
D. To make the parts easier to remove.

mcq.q: What is the primary purpose of laying parts on a clean rag during disassembly?
options: A. To make the parts look neat.
B. To keep the parts clean and organized for reassembly.
C. To protect the parts from scratches.
D. To absorb oil and dust.

mcq.q: Why is it recommended to note the function of each part during disassembly?
options: A. To impress other mechanics.
B. To avoid having extra parts at the end of the job.
C. To speed up the disassembly process.
D. To remember where each part belongs.

mcq.q: What order should the parts be laid out on the rag during disassembly?
options: A. Random order.
B. In alphabetical order.
C. Clockwise order.
D. Counterclockwise order.

mcq.q: According to the excerpt, what should you do if you are worried about forgetting the answers to the questions you ask about each part?
options: A. Skip asking the questions.
B. Rely on memory alone.
C. Make notes.
D. Ask a friend.

mcq.q: According to the text, what is the primary purpose of using a jack?
options: A. To lift a vehicle permanently off the ground.
B. To inspect brakes.
C. To get a vehicle off the ground.
D. To hold a vehicle in place.

mcq.q: Why does the text recommend using jack stands?
options: A. To lift the vehicle higher.
B. To hold the vehicle in place securely.
C. To make it easier to manipulate tools.
D. To improve the vehicle’s stability.

mcq.q: What should you use to block the wheels before jacking up a vehicle?
options: A. Bricks or wooden wedges.
B. Jack stands.
C. Creepers.
D. Cell phones.

mcq.q: If you don't have wheel chocks, what does the text suggest?
options: A. Park near the curb with the wheels turned in.
B. Attempt to find a local auto supply store.
C. Use a jack stand to secure the vehicle.
D. Call road service immediately.

mcq.q: What is the recommended course of action if you need to change a tire on a freeway or highway?
options: A. Proceed with the tire change cautiously.
B. Call road service and remain in the vehicle.
C. Quickly change the tire and return to driving.
D. Signal for help by hanging a white rag out of the window.

mcq.q: According to the excerpt, what is the MOST important thing to do before jacking up a vehicle?
options: A. Removing the wheel cover.
B. Parking on level ground.
C. Loosening the lug nuts.
D. Turning the gearshift to Park.

mcq.q: What should you do if you get a flat tire on a hill and cannot coast to the bottom?
options: A. Immediately jack up the vehicle.
B. Park close to the curb, turn the wheels toward the curb, and block the downside wheels.
C. Attempt to drive to a level area as quickly as possible.
D. Ignore the hill and continue to change the tire.

mcq.q: When is it acceptable to NOT have the parking brake on while working on a vehicle?
options: A. When parking on a hill.
B. When rotating a rear wheel or removing rear brake drums.
C. When changing a tire.
D. When the gearshift is in Park.

mcq.q: Why is it important to loosen the lug nuts *before* jacking up the vehicle?
options: A. To make removing the wheel cover easier.
B. To ensure proper jack placement.
C. Because it's harder to turn the nuts when the wheel is off the ground.
D. To prevent damage to the brakes.

mcq.q: According to the excerpt, where should you place a jack?
options: A. Anywhere convenient.
B. On something that can bend, break or give.
C. Under the part of the vehicle that it should contact when raised.
D. Directly on the frame of the vehicle.

mcq.q: If a vehicle's manual lacks jack placement information, where is it suggested to place the jack?
options: A. Under the rear-wheel axle only.
B. Anywhere convenient.
C. Touching either the vehicle’s frame or the big bar that supports the front wheel suspension.
D. Directly under the vehicle's engine.

mcq.q: According to the excerpt, what is the recommended technique for using a hydraulic jack?
options: A. Using quick, jerky movements.
B. Using nice, even strokes from its lowest point to its highest point.
C. Using a hammer to force it upwards.
D. Only using it to lift the front of the vehicle.

mcq.q: What is strongly discouraged as a substitute for jack stands?
options: A. A second jack.
B. Scissor Jacks.
C. Boxes, stones, or bricks.
D. Hydraulic Jacks.

mcq.q: What is the suggested action to take after placing the jack stands?
options: A. Immediately begin working on the vehicle.
B. Lower the jack until the vehicle is resting on the jack stands.
C. Tighten the jack stands with a wrench.
D. Remove the jack stands.

mcq.q: Why is it recommended to wiggle the vehicle after placing it on jack stands?
options: A. To align the wheels.
B. To ensure it's resting securely and to check wheel blocking.
C. To test the strength of the jack stands.
D. To lubricate the jack stands.

mcq.q: According to the text, what should you do if you have a flat tire and cannot get a cell phone signal?
options: A. Drive slowly until you find help.
B. Attempt to change the tire yourself.
C. Leave the vehicle and seek help.
D. Wait for someone to pass by.

mcq.q: When lowering a vehicle after using a hydraulic jack, what should you use?
options: A. A crank.
B. A lever.
C. A rod to turn the pressure release valve.
D. Your hands.

mcq.q: After securing the vehicle, what is the second step in changing a tire according to the text?
options: A. Removing the old tire.
B. Putting on the new tire.
C. Jacking up the vehicle.
D. Putting away the jack stuff.

mcq.q: According to the excerpt, what is the first task in changing a flat tire?
options: Tightening the lug nuts
Removing the spare tire
Removing the wheel cover or hubcap
Checking the spare tire's air pressure

mcq.q: What tools can be used to pry off a standard wheel cover or hubcap?
options: A hammer and a wrench
A screwdriver or the flat end of a lug wrench
An adjustable wrench and pliers
A crowbar and a jack

mcq.q: Where can you find a list of the tools needed to change a tire?
options: Chapter 1
Chapter 3
The owner's manual
Part II: Vehicle Maintenance

mcq.q: What should you do with the removed wheel cover or hubcap?
options: Discard it
Store it in the trunk
Lay it on its back
Give it to a mechanic

mcq.q: If a wheel cover has a delicate finish, what should you consult?
options: Chapter 3
Figure 1-3
The owner's manual
A local mechanic

mcq.q: What is the primary concern when handling lug nuts with delicate finishes?
options: A. Tightening them to the manufacturer's specifications
B. Avoiding scratching the finish
C. Ensuring they are made of aluminum
D. Replacing them with standard lug nuts

mcq.q: Why should power tools generally not be used to loosen or replace lug nuts with delicate finishes?
options: A. They are too expensive.
B. They can scratch the finish.
C. They are not readily available.
D. They are heavier than hand tools.

mcq.q: What is the purpose of locking nuts on alloy wheels?
options: A. To improve the wheel's appearance.
B. To increase the wheel's speed.
C. To discourage wheel theft.
D. To enhance the wheel's aerodynamics.

mcq.q: According to the text, what tool do many mechanics prefer to use when handling lug nuts with delicate finishes?
options: A. A power wrench
B. A cross-shaft lug wrench
C. A special lug fitting
D. A socket wrench

mcq.q: Why does the text suggest trying to remove the lug nuts on your vehicle now?
options: A. To show off to friends.
B. To prepare for a road trip.
C. To resolve any problems before an emergency.
D. To save money on a mechanic's bill.

mcq.q: According to the excerpt, why are longer arms on a lug wrench beneficial?
options: A. They are easier to store.
B. They provide more leverage.
C. They are lighter in weight.
D. They require less force to use.

mcq.q: Before jacking up a vehicle, what should be done?
options: A. Loosen the lug nuts completely.
B. Replace the flat tire with the spare.
C. Remove the spare tire from the trunk.
D. Tighten the lug nuts by hand.

mcq.q: What should you do after lifting the spare tire onto the lug bolts?
options: A. Lower the vehicle to the ground immediately.
B. Replace the lug nuts and tighten them by hand.
C. Remove the flat tire from under the vehicle.
D. Inspect the jack stands for damage.

mcq.q: According to the text, what should you do after replacing the jack?
options: A. Immediately tighten the lug nuts with the wrench.
B. Use it to lift the vehicle off the jack stands.
C. Store the jack in the trunk.
D. Replace the spare tire with the flat tire.

mcq.q: What is the recommended method for tightening lug nuts on alloy wheels?
options: A. Jerk the wrench forcefully.
B. Jump on the wrench’s cross arm.
C. Press down on the wrench's cross arm with your weight.
D. Kick the wrench to maximize force.

mcq.q: According to the text, what should you do if a wheel cover has a delicate finish?
options: A. Ignore the instructions and replace it yourself
B. Consult the owner's manual for instructions
C. Pry it off and hope it goes back on
D. Whack it with the heel of your hand

mcq.q: When replacing a hubcap, which of the following is recommended?
options: A. Use a wrench or hammer to secure it
B. Cushion your hand with a rag before hitting it
C. Skip replacing it to save time
D. Secure it with strong adhesive

mcq.q: What is the primary concern regarding a service station simply plugging a flat tire from the outside?
options: A. It's the cheapest option
B. It's a procedure outlawed in some states
C. It takes less than a minute to do
D. It’s the most durable fix

mcq.q: According to the text, what is the purpose of a can of nonflammable inflator/sealant?
options: A. To permanently repair a flat tire
B. To get you rolling again in an emergency
C. To replace a damaged hubcap
D. To improve the vehicle’s gas mileage

mcq.q: What should you tell a service facility after using a can of nonflammable inflator/sealant?
options: A. You want them to plug the tire from the outside
B. You want them to install a new hubcap
C. You used canned inflator/sealant and they should remove it before fixing the tire
D. You’d prefer run-flat tires

mcq.q: According to the text, what is the best method for hiding an extra key?
options: A. Taping it to the underside of the vehicle
B. Hiding it under the floor mat
C. Using a magnetic key box
D. Placing it in the glove compartment

mcq.q: What is a potential downside to having electronic door openers?
options: A. They are less secure than traditional keys
B. They require frequent battery replacements
C. Losing the gadget can be costly and time-consuming
D. They are not compatible with all vehicles

mcq.q: If a vehicle has electronic door openers and the opener is lost, what is a possible solution?
options: A. A locksmith can easily replace it
B. The vehicle can be started with a traditional key
C. There might be an override switch
D. The vehicle will automatically unlock

mcq.q: What is suggested if a vehicle has recessed door locks?
options: A. Attempt to pick the lock
B. Use a coat hanger to open it
C. Seek professional help
D. Try a slim jim

mcq.q: According to the excerpt, what can you do if you’ve locked yourself out and the trunk is open?
options: A. Break a window
B. Move the rear seat and access the rear of the car
C. Call a towing service
D. Attempt to pick the lock with a wire hanger

true_false.q: The excerpt implies that some people are born with a natural talent for fixing cars.
The excerpt suggests that beginners and experienced mechanics alike should pay attention to safety.
According to the excerpt, the Introduction details a positive experience related to automobiles.
True or False: The author believes it's always best to do things yourself to save money.
True or False: According to the text, strength is primarily determined by physical size.
True or False: The author recommends turning over tasks to a professional if they fail to pass the list of questions.
The excerpt suggests that aftermarket parts are always superior to OEM parts.
The author frequently had to exchange parts due to ordering the wrong ones.
It is recommended to keep a copy of your Specifications Record in your glove compartment.
True or False: It is safe to work on a vehicle without the parking brake engaged.
True or False: The excerpt suggests that making a mistake while working on a car will always result in damage.
True or False: It is advisable to store gasoline unless you are traveling a long distance.
True or False: Mechanic's solvent is not recommended due to safety concerns.
True or False: Pushing on a wrench is generally safer than pulling on it when working on a stuck part.
True or False: Gasoline should be stored in any container as long as it's airtight.
True or False: Long hair poses a safety risk during vehicle maintenance.
True or False: The text suggests that auto repair safety relies heavily on specialized knowledge rather than common sense.
True or False:  Chapter 3 provides information regarding reasons for keeping a fire extinguisher nearby.
It is safe to stand close to the gas pump while it is filling your tank.
Gas pumps automatically shut off when your tank is almost full.
Chapter 17 provides instructions for checking tire air pressure.
True or False: The location of the hood release is exactly the same on all vehicle models.
True or False: According to the text, checking your own fluids and belts is preferable to asking a gas station attendant to do it.
True or False: The excerpt suggests that ignoring the need to open the hood is a safe and practical option.
True or False: The excerpt advises working on a task quickly to avoid wasting time.
True or False: Hood shocks are used to secure the hood if it doesn't stay up on its own.
True or False: The safety catch is used to lubricate the wheel bearings.
True or False:  The excerpt suggests taking the phone off the hook to avoid distractions.
True or False: If parts fit together before, they will fit together again.
True or False: The excerpt recommends always tightening bolts as tightly as possible to prevent parts from coming loose.
True or False: The recommended disassembly system is designed to help avoid having leftover parts at the end of the job.
True or False: The excerpt suggests using masking tape to number parts if there's a risk of the rag being moved.
True or False: The process described in the excerpt is suitable for complex engine overhauls requiring precise torque specifications.
True or False: When reassembling, you should begin with the first part you removed.
Jacks are intended to hold a vehicle in place while performing maintenance.
Working underneath a vehicle without jack stands is safe as long as you are thin enough to fit.
Wheel chocks are a relatively inexpensive item readily available at auto supply stores.
The text advises changing a flat tire on a freeway or highway.
A basic cellphone is unnecessary if you can hang a white rag from your window to signal for help.
True or False: It is safe to place a jack on something that can bend, break, or give.
True or False:  If you have a manual transmission, you should put the gearshift in First before jacking up the vehicle.
True or False: The excerpt suggests that jacking up a car is a completely risk-free activity.
True or False: The excerpt states that it's unnecessary to remove the wheel cover before jacking up the vehicle.
True or False:  If you're not sure where to place a jack, it is okay to consult your owner's manual or a service department.
It is safe to use boxes or stones as substitutes for jack stands.
Using even strokes when operating a hydraulic jack can reduce the effort required.
It is recommended to remove the jack after the vehicle is secured on jack stands.
A bouncing motion is ideal when a vehicle is resting on jack stands.
Information regarding jack stand purchasing can be found in Chapter 3.
